jungle_temple.name: Jungle Temple
how_to_find: Search jungle biomes; temples are partly hidden by leaves and terrain.
Use /locate structure minecraft:jungle_pyramid if commands are allowed.
Chunkbase/seed maps can help if external tools are allowed.
notes: Contains tripwire traps; tripwire hooks can be part of the trap setup and/or chest loot depending on version/loot table.
Not a reliable bulk source for tripwire hooks compared to crafting.

pillager_outpost.name: Pillager Outpost
how_to_find: Explore open overworld biomes; outposts have a tall watchtower.
Use /locate structure minecraft:pillager_outpost if commands are allowed.
Villages can sometimes be near outposts, but this is not guaranteed.
variants: watchtower with surrounding cages/targets/log piles/tents depending on generation
notes: Can be dangerous because pillagers respawn nearby.
Loot is not a high-volume source of tripwire hooks.

bastion_remnant.name: Bastion Remnant
how_to_find: Explore the Nether except basalt deltas; bastions do not generate there.
Look for large blackstone structures.
Use /locate structure minecraft:bastion_remnant if commands are allowed.
notes: Piglins become hostile if you open chests or mine gold nearby unless handled carefully.
Treasure room variant is usually the best for high-value loot.

village.name: Village
how_to_find: Explore flat/open biomes and follow paths/lamps/houses.
Use /locate structure minecraft:village_* if commands are allowed.
Cartographer maps do not directly point to villages.
notes: Village loot depends on house type/profession building.
Villages are mainly important for villager trades and beds/workstations, not for tripwire hooks.

ancient_city.name: Ancient City
how_to_find: Search deep dark biomes, often under mountain ranges.
Use /locate structure minecraft:ancient_city if commands are allowed.
Move carefully around sculk shriekers to avoid summoning the Warden.
notes: High-risk, high-value loot structure.
Not intended as an early-game source.

mineshaft.name: Mineshaft
how_to_find: Explore caves; mineshafts contain wooden supports, rails, and cobwebs.
Badlands have a higher chance of exposed mineshafts.
Use /locate structure minecraft:mineshaft if commands are allowed.
notes: Good source of rails, cobwebs/string, and cave spider spawners.

stronghold.name: Stronghold
how_to_find: Throw eyes of ender and follow their direction.
Strongholds generate underground in rings around spawn.
Use /locate structure minecraft:stronghold if commands are allowed.
notes: Primary progression structure for reaching the End portal.

desert_pyramid.name: Desert Pyramid
how_to_find: Explore desert biomes; pyramids are sandstone structures.
Avoid stepping on the central pressure plate above TNT.
Use /locate structure minecraft:desert_pyramid if commands are allowed.
notes: Good early treasure source but not renewable.
Loot table changes with archaeology/version.

shipwreck.name: Shipwreck
how_to_find: Explore oceans and beaches by boat.
Dolphins can lead to nearby underwater ruins or shipwreck-related treasure.
Use /locate structure minecraft:shipwreck if commands are allowed.
notes: One of the best ways to find buried treasure maps.

buried_treasure.name: Buried Treasure
how_to_find: Use buried treasure maps from shipwrecks or ocean ruins.
Dig near the X marker, usually under sand/gravel near beaches.
notes: Primary source of Heart of the Sea.

end_city.name: End City
how_to_find: Defeat the Ender Dragon, enter an end gateway, then bridge/fly across outer End islands.
Look for tall purpur structures; ships generate near some cities.
notes: Elytra only appears in End ships, not every End City.
Shulkers are the renewable-ish reason to revisit multiple cities for shells.

nether_fortress.name: Nether Fortress
how_to_find: Explore Nether regions; long bridges and nether brick structures indicate fortresses.
Moving along one axis can help because fortress generation is region-based.
Use /locate structure minecraft:fortress if commands are allowed.
notes: Blaze spawners and nether wart make this a key progression structure.

trial_chambers.name: Trial Chambers
how_to_find: Use trial explorer maps from cartographers when available.
Explore underground deepslate layers.
Use /locate structure minecraft:trial_chambers if commands are allowed.
notes: Loot depends on vault/trial spawner mechanics and version.
Good source of trial-specific items in modern versions.
